title: Universal Physics book manifest
description: Schema for book/manifest.json — the build manifest consumed by book/build.js. Content policy: source .md pages are FROZEN; the manifest only declares structure (parts, chapters, sources) and trim (chrome-stripping) instructions. This schema is for editor/tooling validation; the build path uses plain JS (scripts/check-book-manifest.mjs) and adds no runtime validation dependency.

$schema.description: Relative path to this schema for editor tooling.

book.description: Top-level book metadata used to render the title/copyright pages.

conventions.description: Project-wide conventions (epistemic tags, content/link policy). Free-form; not consumed structurally by the build.

frontMatter.description: Front-matter pages. Most are generated (id + generate hint); the preface carries a real source path.

parts.description: Ordered parts; each has an opener page and an ordered list of chapters.

opener.description: Path (relative to sourceRoot) to the part-opener markdown.

notes.description: Optional editorial note for this part (e.g. how working-note links are cited). Not rendered structurally.

sources.description: One or more source .md paths (relative to sourceRoot) concatenated into the chapter. build.js renders sources[0] as the chapter spine; multi-source chapters run their sources in order.

trim.description: Human-readable chrome-stripping instruction. Advisory; the actual stripping logic lives in build.js.

backMatter.description: Back-matter sections (glossary, bibliography, about, publication history).

items.description: A back-matter entry. Source ASYMMETRY (matched in build.js): most entries carry a plural `sources` array, but the 'about' entry carries a singular `source` string. Exactly one of `source`/`sources` is expected for a sourced entry; 'publication-history' is generated from a condensed CHANGELOG and may rely on build.js logic. This schema allows either form.

source.description: Singular source path. Used by the 'about' entry; build.js branches on bm.source vs bm.sources.
